summary: Marcelo Bielsa's Uruguay — aggressive, exciting and chaotic in a 4-3-3, with counter-press goals but a risky rest-defence that concedes late. Bring it on.
style: 4-3-3 under Bielsa — attacking wide & crosses into the box, aggressive build, third-man & combination play, counter-press & counter-attacking goals.

counterReliance.note: Counter-press & counter-attack goals, but aggressive build-first

strengths: Aggressive build and third-man combination play
weaknesses: Risky rest-defence — open to transition goals and conceding late critical goals

keyPlayers.name: Maxi Araújo
keyPlayers.note: 9 goal contributions for Sporting, creative with end product (30 chances created), 4 assists in qualifying

tactics: Classic Bielsa — aggressive, combinative and relentless, winning it back high and breaking fast, at the cost of an open rest-defence. Always among the most exciting, if not always the winners.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
